hyperedges.label: AegisAgent redesign document set (what/why, how, approved look)
One OTP pairing flow for devices and harnesses
AegisOS-Agent Behavioral Mode Variants
hyperedges.rationale: Four prompt files define permission/autonomy variants of the same AegisOS-Agent, differing mainly in how much approval is required before executing actions.

hyperedges.label: Shared TTS + Notification + Security Framework
hyperedges.rationale: Every AegisOS mode reuses an identical trailing block: TTS spoken-summary directive, aegis-notify proactive notifications, and security-restriction handling.

hyperedges.label: Fable-5 Memory and Personalization Subsystem
hyperedges.rationale: These Claude Fable 5 sections jointly govern how prior-conversation memory, past-chat retrieval, and user preferences personalize responses without attribution.

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: AegisAgent (product overview)
nodes.rationale: Local personal assistant framework that operates directly on the host OS within user-configurable security guardrails; glassmorphic dashboard with real-time text and audio interaction and live browser integration.

nodes.label: agent-backend (Node/Express + Security Guard)
nodes.rationale: Boots on 127.0.0.1:6800 internal-only; refuses to start without LITELLM_KEY.

nodes.label: Dashboard (Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind 4)
nodes.rationale: Custom server on :6801 — the only exposed port; proxies /api/* and the WebSocket to the backend.

nodes.label: Lightpanda headless browser
nodes.rationale: Fast lightweight browsing via Docker CDP, wrapped as an MCP server; provides live viewports/screenshots.

nodes.label: Dynamic Security Guard (HITL)
nodes.rationale: Filters file paths and shell commands; risky commands paused for Human-in-the-Loop approval; configured via security-config.json (allowed read/write dirs, allowed utilities, auto-approve, require-approval toggle).

nodes.label: Claude Fable 5 prompt option
nodes.rationale: 1,000+ line behavioral prompt directive, stored as prompts/claude-fable-5.md.

nodes.label: Next.js agent rules (AGENTS.md)
nodes.rationale: Warns this Next.js has breaking changes vs training data; read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code.

nodes.label: dashboard/CLAUDE.md
nodes.rationale: Single include directive: @AGENTS.md.

nodes.label: Dashboard README (create-next-app boilerplate)
nodes.rationale: Unmodified create-next-app scaffold README; flagged by the redesign for rewrite to reflect Next 16 + React 19 + Tailwind 4.

nodes.label: AegisAgent Product Redesign Plan
nodes.rationale: The what/why; dated 2026-07-10; supersedes all earlier plan docs; declares the mock the source of truth for target UI.

nodes.label: Local-first agent-operations console
nodes.rationale: Like Cursor's agent panel but harness-agnostic and self-hosted: harnesses do the work, devices drive it, connectors extend it, skills specialize it, policies govern it, everything observable, voice first-class.

nodes.label: Console surface
nodes.rationale: Sessions list + unified timeline (toggle to Mission view) + contextual inspector + composer.

nodes.label: Fleet surface
nodes.rationale: Pairs devices and harnesses via one OTP flow; harness registry + device registry with revoke. Killed the old 'Paired devices 0' footer row.

nodes.label: Connectors surface
nodes.rationale: Own page for the MCP tool-server registry, moved out of Fleet. Skills = instructions; connectors = tools.

nodes.label: Policies surface
nodes.rationale: The whole authorization story on one screen: capability x mode matrix, budgets, per-device overrides.

nodes.label: Settings surface
nodes.rationale: One page on the rail: models, prompt directives, skills, voice, memory/compaction. Killed the second settings entry point.

nodes.label: Unified timeline
nodes.rationale: One chronological stream — chat woven in, not a separate column. Entry types: user message, plan card, tool call, edit card, reasoning accordion, sub-agent lane, approval gate, assistant message. The work is the centerpiece; replaces chat+hidden-tabs.

nodes.label: Mission view
nodes.rationale: A lens, not a tab: the same session data projected as the agent's own phase breakdown — tasks with owner (main or sub-agent), status, cross-check gates, re-plans tagged with the reasoning turn that introduced them. Deep-effort sessions get it by default; never a second source of truth.

nodes.label: Inspector (Overview/Workspace/Preview/Trace)
nodes.rationale: 348px contextual right panel with four segments; replaces the old 5-tab panel where one tab was clipped offscreen. Overview = usage tiles + tokens-per-turn chart + context/budget meters + sub-agent list + latency; Trace = end-to-end per-sub-agent detail.

nodes.label: Composer (compact)
nodes.rationale: Single-line textarea + one quiet row of uniform accordion chips: mode, harness, prompt, effort, skills, plus dictate mic, TTS toggle, Send.

nodes.label: Prompt library
nodes.rationale: Stored system prompts (standard + claude/gemini/codex frontier-style flavors) selected per session; inherited by the main agent AND every sub-agent it spawns; mode directives appended on top, not mixed in. Add by paste/file/URL.

nodes.label: Permission mode (Chat/Plan/Edit/Yolo)
nodes.rationale: One of two orthogonal session axes — what the agent MAY do.

nodes.label: Effort profile (fast/balanced/deep)
nodes.rationale: Effort != permission. A composer profile = model routing + reasoning budget; deliberately kept orthogonal to permission mode. Two axes, not seven modes. Defaults editable in Settings.

nodes.label: Harness
nodes.rationale: Harnesses do the work — pi-code today, opencode/any CLI tomorrow via an adapter protocol. Local (child process) or remote (WS + token). Multiple per machine, each with its own policy scope.

nodes.label: Device
nodes.rationale: Laptop, phone, tablet — drive the console. Paired via URL + OTP with a scope chosen at pairing (chat+voice / read-only / full control). devices table (id, name, scope, token-hash, created, last_seen); revocation kills token + live sockets.

nodes.label: OTP pairing
nodes.rationale: One flow pairs both devices and harnesses: URL + short-lived (~5 min) OTP; success mints a long-lived token; WS upgrade authenticates it. Scope chosen at pairing time.

nodes.label: aegis-adapter protocol
nodes.rationale: CLI (npx aegis-adapter --code ...) that claims an OTP, opens an authenticated WS, and bridges a remote harness's stdio protocol. Any CLI that speaks it can connect.

nodes.label: Connectors (MCP tool-server registry)
nodes.rationale: MCP servers (command or URL + auth) registered once; tools namespaced connector.tool; exposed to every harness session; every call passes the policy engine and lands in timeline+metrics like any tool. github is the reference connector; lightpanda already exists.

nodes.label: Skills (instruction packs)
nodes.rationale: Directory of instruction packs (skills/<name>/SKILL.md); session-attach injects into the system prompt; always-on flag in Settings. Reusable, per-session or always-on.

nodes.label: Policy engine v2 (capability x mode matrix)
nodes.rationale: Capability x mode matrix (allow/ask/block) replacing the single global config blob and AEGIS_MODE env fallback; scoped per device and per harness with overrides; 'ask' emits an approval_request event; budgets enforced server-side (pause at cap). Hot-reloads per instance.
nodes.norm_label: policy engine v2 (capability x mode matrix)

nodes.label: Budgets & limits (enforced)
nodes.rationale: Enforced not advisory: max cost/session, token budget, sub-agent depth; harness paused at cap with a timeline gate asking to continue.

nodes.label: In-timeline approval gate
nodes.rationale: Approvals happen in place: policy 'ask' renders a gate card in the timeline at the moment the action fires (Approve once / Deny), no modal, no separate queue; only devices with approval rights resolve; resolution broadcast to all viewers.

nodes.label: Sub-agent observability (end-to-end)
nodes.rationale: Every spawn records task, parent, depth; sub-agent reasoning + tools captured; each sub-agent gets its own trace (task, reasoning, tools, metrics) that streams live and is persisted. First-class nested lanes with lineage color.

nodes.label: Provider-reported usage accounting
nodes.rationale: Parse provider usage (prompt/completion tokens) from LiteLLM responses; per-model pricing map -> cost; persisted per turn AND per agent. The word 'estimated' disappears from the UI.

nodes.label: TTS / voice (first-class I/O)
nodes.rationale: Sentence-level TTS pipeline stays; mute stops playback immediately; barge-in (mic activity pauses audio); blob URLs revoked; reasoning and tool output are never spoken.

nodes.label: Design system (from the mock)
nodes.rationale: Dark-committed ops console; ground #0f0f13, accent iris #8781ec; status colors never used as chart series; chart series in #8781ec / out #1baf7a (CVD+contrast validated); monospace is the data voice. Real semantics: role=tab, focus-visible, no @ts-nocheck / props:any.

nodes.label: AegisAgent Implementation Plan
nodes.rationale: The how; companion to REDESIGN-PLAN (what/why) and the mock (approved look). Six phases ordered so each ships something visible; commit per phase, run npm run verify. Phases 1-2 are the core ask; 3-4 change the security model; 5-6 additive; cut 6.3 first.

nodes.label: metrics.js real-usage wiring (Phase 1)
nodes.rationale: Parse LiteLLM usage into metrics.js; char-count heuristic kept only as fallback flagged source:'estimated'; add cost + per-model pricing map to createEmptyMetrics(); per-turn append-only ledger.

nodes.label: SubagentTracker wiring (Phase 1)
nodes.rationale: Register the dead subagent_tool_start/end + reasoning listeners in server.js; invoke SubagentTracker.startToolCall/endToolCall and metrics.addSubagent family (implemented but never called); fix parentId=null hardcoding; serialize toJSON/fromJSON into the session row so traces survive restart.

nodes.label: WS event vocabulary (PROTOCOL.md)
nodes.rationale: One WS event vocabulary feeding the timeline: turn_start, reasoning, tool_start/end, edit, subagent_spawn, subagent_event, approval_request/resolved, usage_update, assistant_message. Documented in agent-backend/PROTOCOL.md.

nodes.label: devices table + pair endpoints (Phase 3)
nodes.rationale: POST /api/pair/start (5-min OTP, owner-only) + /api/pair/claim (code+name+scope -> device token); token check on every HTTP route and WS upgrade replaces the fail-open middleware; scopes chat_voice/read_only/full enforced server-side; revocation kills sockets.

nodes.label: harness_instances registry (Phase 3)
nodes.rationale: harness_instances table (type, transport, machine, policy scope, status); local=spawn, remote=aegis-adapter over authenticated WS; multiple per machine; sessions keyed (deviceId, sessionId); removes kill-sibling-session behavior.

nodes.label: AegisAgent Console Redesign Mock
nodes.rationale: Approved interactive HTML mock and source of truth for the target UI; toggle 'Design notes' for the rationale behind each decision. Implements icon rail with Console/Fleet/Connectors/Policies/Settings views.

nodes.label: Session Metrics Panel (Tokens, Tool Calls, Latency, Cost)
nodes.norm_label: session metrics panel (tokens, tool calls, latency, cost)

nodes.label: Right Panel Tabs (Agent, Workspace, Plan, Logs, Settings)
nodes.norm_label: right panel tabs (agent, workspace, plan, logs, settings)

nodes.label: Chat Composer Input (Send, Voice, Prompt Mode)
nodes.norm_label: chat composer input (send, voice, prompt mode)
